observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

Submit the Monthly and Consolidated RAAFs covering the period of the cash examination;
Immediately present to the Audit Team the above-listed series of Accountable Forms or RCDs of the same for verification; and
Observe careful and timely recording of the collections in the RCD and prepare updated RAAF for accurate recording.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer render his updated cashbooks and henceforth, update all his cashbooks on a daily basis and conduct daily reconciliation of the balances per cashbook as against the actual cash on hand and accounting records.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant regularly prepare a detailed Aging Schedule of Receivables and Schedule Payables based on subsidiary ledgers for proper accounting, internal control and timely enforcement of collections and payment of valid claims against the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of PPE and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and submit the same to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the books. Moreover, submit copies of the RCPPE and reconciliation to the Audit Team.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant correct the Inventory accounts to fairly present the financial statements based on the proposed adjusting journal entries in paragraphs 7.3.2 and 7.3.6;
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant observe careful recording of transactions in consideration of the purposes of procurement and supporting documents to avoid further errors especially on Inventory and PPE accounts and require the implementing office to submit proper documents for the distribution of goods; and
The Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count and prepare Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for inventories on hand at year-end and submit copies to the Audit Team, as well as, the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation purposes.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
The Personnel Officer/Human Resource Officer submit certified copies of the contracts of services and/or job orders for CY 2023;
The Municipal Accountant require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Acknowledgement as received by the employees for those unsigned; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer require the recipients of salaries to sign on the payroll register upon receipt, the Municipal Accountant allow succeeding cash advances only when the previous cash advance was liquidated and demand liquidation thereof, and the Personnel Officer/Human Resource Officer submit to COA all contracts of services and/or job orders at the beginning of the year.
We recommended and Management agreed to furnish the Audit Team delivery documents of goods and services within 24 hours after acceptance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the abovementioned disbursement vouchers for audit; otherwise, Management cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of officials concerned until compliance with the requirements of the Commission, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against such official or employee pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended that the Members of the BAC submit to the Municipal Accountant perfected contracts and purchase orders and consequently the Municipal Accountant submit said documents to the Audit Team within the reglementary period.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Submit the noted lacking documents to establish the validity of the disbursements; and
Henceforth, ensure that all claims are supported with sufficient, relevant and completely accomplished documents prior submission to the Municipal Treasurer for payments to claimant/supplier; and the Municipal Treasurer process only claims that are duly certified as complete.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Municipal Engineer as the acting Building Official indicate in every Order of Payment the breakdown of total collections for building permits and other charges the 80% share of the Municipality and the 20% share of the national government {15% Office of the Building Official (OBO) and 5% DPWH}; and
Municipal Treasurer establish the 15% share of the OBO and 5% share of the DPWH, and transfer thereafter the share of the OBO to its TF account and remit the share of the DPWH to the special account of the DPWH thru the Bureau of Treasury and show in the official Receipts of collections of building permits and other charges the breakdown of the shares of the LGU, OBO and DPWH.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor immediately require all concerned personnel to prepare a status on the actions taken on audit findings and recommendations in the CY 2022 AAR (part II and part III) as of year-end and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for consolidation through the AAPSI form and consequently be submitted to the Audit Team; and henceforth, ensure timely submission of the duly accomplished AAPSI within 60 days from receipt of the AAR and after end of each year.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit the MOA for the fund transfers from DOLE for TUPAD of ₱2,342,210.00 and PGA of ₱15,000,000.00 for audit.

We recommended that Management:
Refrain lump-sum appropriation to prevent over disbursements and mix-up of funds and limit disbursements with the amount of appropriation; and
Prepare correct Fund Utilization and Status reports in accordance with the prescribed form to ensure understanding by users and reliable reporting.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the supporting documents from the Municipal Agriculturist, for the entry made for the distributed farm equipment, fertilizers and pesticides - duly accomplished acknowledgement receipt of recipients (indicated therein the specifications and quantity of goods, and date of actual distribution);
Henceforth, Municipal Accountant ensure that entries made in the JEV or journals for the recording of the expenses for goods distributed are duly supported with SSMI, RIS, pictures and acknowledgement receipts from the Municipal Treasurer/GSO; and
Municipal Treasurer/GSO and Municipal Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards, respectively, as required in Sections 119 and 120 of Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs to have permanent and immediate references of all procurement and distribution of items to the end-users/beneficiaries.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor as chairman of the LSB ensure approval of annual SEF budget; and
Public School District Supervisor as Co-Chairman of the LSB prepare annual SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1/Income and Expenditure Estimate in accordance with the guidelines, supported with the Local Development Investment Program (LDIP) and Annual Investment Program.

We recommended that:
Management determine the amount that could still be transferred to the 20% Development Fund account through review of the realignments from the prior years and projects implemented not included in the AIP;
The Municipal Treasurer observe regular transfer of the 20% Development Fund to its depository account to further avoid discrepancy and misuse of cash; and
The Municipal Budget Officer strictly monitor the budget and expenditures through maintenance of an updated Statement of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (SAAOB) to avoid possible misuse of funds.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LDRRM Officer prepare the annual LDRRMFIP and monthly LDRRMF utilization report using the prescribed form to provide complete and detailed information on the plan and report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Accountant to submit the acknowledgement receipt/distribution list for the procurement of the subject relief goods;
Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer as GSO and the Municipal Accountant to observe the guidelines on the accounting, receipt and issuance of supplies, materials and goods for distribution as prescribed by pertinent rules and regulations by using RIS and keeping stock cards and Supplies Ledger Cards; and
Focal point person for distribution to record issuances through the sufficiently detailed acknowledgement receipt/distribution list submitted immediately to the Municipal Treasurer/GSO.

We recommended that Management present the Authorization to Purchase Motor Vehicle for the two motor vehicles (pick-up trucks) by the DILG-CAR Region Director to the Municipality of Sallapadan and submit a certified true copy, if any.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the unspent LDRRMF as of December 31, 2023 amounting ₱1,721,263.06 to avoid possible mismanagement of the fund and the Municipal Accountant record the same using the prescribed entries in paragraph 24.12 to the special trust fund account to enable proper monitoring.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that GPB for succeeding years are reviewed and endorsed by the MPDO and DILG Provincial Office to ascertain GAD-responsiveness of the PPAs and alignment with the provincial priorities.
Submit the GAD AR, based on the DILG-endorsed GPB, in its prescribed format and accompanied with the required reports/documents; and
Ensure implementation in accordance with the DILG-endorsed GPB by the responsible offices and for changes, acquire approval from the Local Sanggunian and comply with the submission to the DILG Provincial Office for additional review.

We recommended that the Municipal SWMB headed by the Municipal Mayor:
Direct coordination of LGU personnel concerned in the integration of the PPAs in the SWMP to the LGU’s AIP, their implementation and adoption of specific revenue-generating measures for the SWMP; and
Regularly monitor the status of the SWMP, review the deficiencies and ensure its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant record the proposed adjustments in items 28.4 and 28.6 for the noted errors to correct the balance of the account Due to BIR and other related accounts; and henceforth, observe prudence in recording in the books; and
Municipal Treasurer remit immediately to the BIR the overdue taxes of ₱75,654.50; inquire to BIR on alternative measure for the over remittance; and henceforth, promptly remit the correct amount of taxes withheld for the succeeding years within the prescribed period per Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012.

observations.recommendation:
1.12 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1.12.1 Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to locate the records
where the necessary data can be sourced to complete the details and
specifications of the roads and their components to facilitate in the
preparation and maintenance of the required records and reports
and computation of depreciation;
1.12.2 Inventory Committee to conduct a yearly physical count of local
road network projects and accordingly update the RPCLRN and
local road map of the Municipality;
1.12.3 Personnel concerned to prepare and maintain the required records
and reports for all roads and their components – Registry of Public
Infrastructures for Roads, Subsidiary Ledgers, Local Road Network
Property Cards, Local Road Inventory, Road Map, Report on Local
Road Network and year-end RPCLRN; and
1.12.4 Municipal Accountant to include in the Notes to Financial
Statements necessary details of the local roads recorded in the books
as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25,
2015.

2.6 We have recommended the following to ascertain the fairness of the
recorded balance of PPE:
2.6.1 Municipal Mayor ensure the conduct of annual physical count of
PPE by the Inventory Committee and subsequent preparation and
submission of the RPCPPE to the Accounting Office and Audit
Team; and
2.6.2 Municipal Accountant reconcile the RPCPPE with the books of the
Municipality and verify variances for adjustments as necessary.

4.7.6 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
4.7.6.1 Demand immediate return of the unliquidated cash advance
of ₱10,000.00 by the said officer; and
4.7.6.2 Henceforth, strictly observe the rules and regulations on the
granting of cash advances to those duly appointed or
designated disbursing officers.

4.8.6 We have recommended that Management observe the rules and
regulations on the granting of cash advances and duly support them
with complete documentary requirements as provided under Item 4.2
of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

4.9.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the
necessary supporting documents for the payments of salaries and
wages –
4.9.5.1 Contracts of services, accomplishment reports for job orders
and contractual workers, and other pertinent documents for
those payments of Other General Services and Other
Professional Services; and
4.9.5.2 Acknowledgement as received by the employees for those
unsigned and authorization letter and certified true copy of
identification cards of representatives for those received by
persons other than the recipients.

4.9.6 Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer require the recipients of
honoraria, salaries and wages and similar payments to sign on the
payroll register upon receipt, and in cases of representatives to
submit authorization letter on behalf of the recipients named in the
payroll and certified true copy of the representative’s identification
card.

5.12 We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
5.12.1 Deposit immediately the accumulated collections amounting to
₱563,680.63 and;
5.12.2 Henceforth, deposit collections regularly and intact, pursuant to the
above-mentioned rules and regulations.

6.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
6.5.1 Render immediately the updated cashbooks; and
6.5.2 Henceforth, observe daily recording of transactions in the
cashbooks and monthly footing of balances.

7.19 We have recommended that the
Municipal Accountant:
7.19.1 Submit immediately the noted lacking documents to establish the
propriety and validity of the disbursements; and
7.19.2 Henceforth, ensure that all documentary requirements supporting
all procurement and disbursements are present prior payment to
claimant/supplier.

8.11 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure timely
submission from offices concerned of documents, records and reports to
the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes to meet the prescribed
submission date and enforce suspension of salaries for non-submission.

9.5 We have recommended that Management:
9.5.1 Submit immediately the contract agreement and complete bidding
documents of the discussed projects;
9.5.2 Henceforth, ensure that all perfected contracts and purchase orders
are submitted to COA within five days after perfection thereof for the
immediate review of the reasonableness of the cost, conduct of
inspection and other necessary audit procedures.

10.6 We have recommended that Management immediately submit the list of all
implemented and on-going projects of the Municipality for CY 2021 and
henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the
above-mentioned rules and regulations.

11.9 We have recommended that Management:
11.9.1 Submit the noted procurement documents; and
11.9.2 Henceforth, comply on the prescribed guidelines set for the
procurement through alternative modes.

12.3.7 We have recommended that:
12.3.7.1 Management submit the supplemental APP and
supporting PPMPs of the above-noted procurement of
goods and infrastructure projects for audit purposes, if
any and reason for delayed or non-submission of said
documents; and
12.3.7.2 Henceforth, the members of the BAC ensure to undertake

regardless of funding source in accordance with an
approved APP and require the Head of the BAC
Secretariat to furnish COA of the certification on posting

ABCs of the noted procurements.

12.6.7.2 Henceforth, perform detailed engineering activities before
bidding and prepare complete detailed engineering
documents to support the infrastructure projects.

12.7 We have recommended the members of the BAC ensure that
complete bidding documents using the prescribed edition by GPPB

Bid until the deadline of submission of bids.

12.8.10 We have recommended that the members of the BAC:
12.8.10.1 Submit the noted eligibility requirements for the
mentioned projects; and
12.8.10.2 Henceforth, ensure complete submission of eligibility

No. 9184 by all bidders and duly attached in the bidding

establish the validity and propriety of the contract
awards and payments.

12.9.8 We have recommended that the members of the BAC ensure that

to entering into contract.

12.10.3 We have recommended that the BAC Secretariat comply with the
posting requirements of the Notice of Award, contract agreement
and Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS and agency website, if any,
within the reglementary period as required by the Revised IRR of
R.A. No. 9184.

13.4 We have recommended that Management submit immediately the
documentary requirements as mentioned under the DBM LBM No. 81
relative to the share from Tobacco Excise Tax in CY 2020 for evaluation of
the fund utilization.

14.10 We have recommended that Management:
14.10.1 Submit copies of the Memorandum of Agreement and SB
Resolution for the above-mentioned fund transfers for
verification purposes; and
14.10.2 Henceforth, fully support all transactions which involve funds
from other government agencies with a Memorandum of
Agreement or any appropriate document and SB Resolution
authorizing the Local Chief Executive to enter into such
agreements.

15.8 We have recommended that the:
15.8.1 Municipal Mayor as chairman of the LSB ensure approval of
annual SEF budget; and
15.8.2 Public School District Supervisor as Co-Chairman of the LSB
prepare annual Income and Expenditure Estimate in accordance

16.11 We have recommended that the:
16.11.1 Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal
Engineer submit Fund Utilization/Accomplishment Report with

appropriation of the 20% Development Fund as of December 31,

16.11.2 Municipal Mayor ensure the implementation of the development

completion of the on-going projects funded from continuing
appropriations of the 20% Development Fund.

17.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
17.5.1 Transfer immediately the amount of ₱5,473,261.20 remaining in the
general fund depository bank to the separate bank account of the
20% Development Fund for proper monitoring; and
17.5.2 Henceforth, observe regular transferring of funds to the
Municipality’s 20% Development Fund depository bank in
compliance with R.A. No. 7160 and avoid discrepancy of cash and
risk of mishandling of funds.

18.6 We have recommended that the Municipal DRRMO prepare the annual
LDRRMFIP and monthly LDRRMF utilization report using the prescribed
form to provide complete and detailed information on the plan and report.

19.10 We have recommended that Management:
19.10.1 Submit certified true copies of the approved authority to purchase
motor vehicle by the DILG-CAR Region Director to the LGU-
Sallapadan relative to the procured the rescue vehicle; and
19.10.2 Review the latest applicable DILG Memorandum Circular for
guidance on further procurements of motor vehicles.

20.4.10 We have recommended that Management ensure that POs are
completely accomplished - indicate therein the basic data that a PO
must contain as set forth in the guidelines of COA Circular No. 96-
010 dated August 15, 1996, to establish the legality and propriety
of the transactions and to prevent possible government loss in case
of inconsistencies of goods delivered.

20.5.5 We have recommended that the:
20.5.5.1 Municipal Accountant submit immediately at least one of
the listed lacking documentary requirements noted in
paragraph 20.5.4; and
20.5.5.2 Henceforth, Municipal Mayor ensure that emergency
procurements (negotiated) applicable to GPPB Circular No.
01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 are in accordance with the its
provisions and that documents to establish the legality and
propriety of the transactions are complete before payments.

20.6.10 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
20.6.10.1 Municipal Social Welfare Development Office
(MSWDO), which is tasked as the focal department in
the identification and distribution of relief goods to
beneficiaries, to submit immediately the required
Distribution List in accordance with COA Circular No.
2014-002 dated April 15, 2014, and ensure the submission
of the same for future transactions, together with the RIS
upon issuances for distribution to beneficiaries of relief
125
goods for proper control and monitoring thereof;
20.6.10.2 Municipal Accountant to submit all other documentary
requirements in the procurement of the subject relief
goods and to prepare and maintain their respective
Supplies Ledger Cards;
20.6.10.3 Municipal Treasurer as GSO prepare and maintain an
inventory list of all procured items before repacking of
goods and corresponding Stock Cards for purposes of
check and balance and provide complete
data/information in the AIR and RIS and conduct
periodic reconciliation of records with the accounting
office.

21.6 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the
proposed adjusting entry as presented in paragraph 21.6.3 of this
observation and ensure to follow the accounting and reporting
guidelines set forth in the COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012.

21.7 We have recommended that the:
21.7.7.1 Municipal Treasurer deposit the unexpended LDRRMF as
of December 31, 2021 amounting ₱1,103,786.41 to safeguard
the fund from mismanagement; and
21.7.7.2 Municipal Accountant record the proposed entries in
paragraph 19.7.6 upon deposit of the unspent LDRRMF to
the special trust fund account to enable proper monitoring.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1.12.1 Municipal Engineer and the Municipal General Services Officer
(Municipal Treasurer) conduct a yearly physical count of local road
network projects and accordingly maintain an update local road
map of the MLGU;
1.12.2 Municipal General Services Officer (Municipal Treasurer) to
maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a
complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its
components;
1.12.3 Municipal Accountant to trace the recorded road, highways and
bridges which were closed to government equity prior to 2016 and
subsequently record in its books of accounts the amount of road
works equivalent to the required transition provision, keep and
maintain Registry of Public Infrastructures for Road and subsidiary
records for roads and road components for every road network and
provide for the depreciation for the local road network;
1.12.4 Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects; and
1.12.5 Municipal Accountant to include in the Notes to Financial
Statements necessary details of the local roads recorded in the books
as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We have recommended that the:
2.6.1 Municipal Mayor ensure the conduct of annual physical count of PPE
by the Inventory Committee and subsequent preparation and
submission of the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the
Accounting Office and Audit Team; and
2.6.2 Municipal Accountant reconcile the RPCPPE with the books of the

segregation of road components for road projects.

review the COA Circular 2015-008 for the reporting guidelines of the
Local Road Network to provide reliable data on the said account.

yearly inventory taking and prepare and submit the Report on Physical
Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team and Accounting Office; and
3.6.2 Municipal Accountant reconcile the RPCPPE with the books of the LGU
and verify variances for subsequent adjustments as necessary.

furnish the Audit Team) to record the reconciling items in the accounting
books to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account in the
financial statements;
4.8.2 Municipal Treasurer (MT) to record in the cashbook the identified
reconciling item to reconcile its records with the accounting; and
4.8.3 MA and MT to trace back transactions in the prior years to determine the
causes of the noted discrepancies and adjust the appropriate book; and
4.8.4 Henceforth, the accounting, treasury and the bank records should be
reconciled monthly pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 to ensure that
all transactions affecting Cash and its related accounts are timely,
accurately and completely recorded and reported for a reliable basis in
decision making.

In this regard, create an Inventory Committee to oversee the actual inventory taking procedures and reconcile the report derived therein with the balance shown in the books of accounts. Also, submit immediately the contract documents of the above noted projects totaling ₱19,748,357.85 to COA for evaluation.

13.7	In view of the noted violations we have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the required documentary requirements and henceforth all fund transfers to LGUs should be supported by Memorandum of Agreement.
13.8	Moreover, we have recommended that liquidations for the ₱1,728,938.78 be also submitted to the audit team for evaluation.

14.9	We have recommended that the management submit a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement to the audit team in order to verify whether the specific purpose for which the fund was established was met. Henceforth, all transactions which involve funds from other Government Agencies should be fully supported with a Memorandum of Agreement or any appropriate document.

15.7	We have recommended that management submit justification on the noted deficiencies. Also, we have recommended that management compel the contractor to refund the amount of ₱1,641,420.28 in the event that full payment was already made to the contractor. In case there was no other payments yet made to the contractor, the management may withhold the payments to succeeding progress billing and compel the contractor to refund the excess of ₱2,585.51. Otherwise, any overpayment shall be personally borne by the officials who will be named accountable persons in the corresponding Notice of Disallowance that will soon be issued.
18.7	We have recommended that the agency submit a copy of an approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for CY 2016 and henceforth, an APP should be prepared and approved before any procurement is made.

19.11	In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended the following:
That management justify the above noted lapses and submit the missing documents for evaluation;
That henceforth, the BAC conducts post-qualification and strictly follow the “pass/fail” criteria in the conduct of bidding and automatically disqualify non-complying bidders; and
That management strictly comply with the regulations set forth under RA 9184, its implementing rules and regulations and promulgations by the GPPB.

20.8	We have recommended that Management require the contractors to post additional warranty security for those which are still within the 1-year defects liability period. Henceforth, oblige all contractors to post the required warranty securities.

21.9	We have recommended that management consider the default mode of procurement and that is through competitive public bidding. Purchases through shopping or small value procurement shall be within the threshold prescribed under the IRR of RA 9184.
21.10	Moreover, we have recommended that the Municipality consider purchasing new motor vehicles or construction and heavy equipment to avoid excessive repairs.

22.8	In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended that management justify the noted deficiencies and compute the applicable liquidated damages and imposed the same to the contractor of the project: Finishes and Extension of Multi-Purpose Building.

23.5	We have recommended that expenditures from the Special Education Fund be supported with the corresponding Municipal School Board Budget. Henceforth, the management should follow strictly the pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160 before disbursement from the Special Education Fund is made.

24.4	In view of the noted violations, we have recommended the following:
The Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution properly authorizing the Municipality of Sallapadan to open a separate bank account for the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax. Further, maintain a separate book for the said fund as well as establish a complete set of subsidiary ledgers to account for its transactions; and
Submit justification on the non-implementation of projects appropriated under the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax and utilization of the same for other purposes not intended for the said fund.

27.8	We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution or Administrative Order creating the LCPC and Annual Work and Financial Plan for CY 2015, if any. Henceforth, observe and abide with the rules and regulations with regard to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.

28.5	We have recommended that the municipality submit justification on the utilization of funds to other activities intended for the payment of current liabilities and implementation of programs under the LDRRMF, 20% development fund and share from tobacco excise tax. We also have recommended that the municipality carefully monitor the cash balance in order to avoid incurrence of overdraft.

29.5	We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer promptly remit all taxes withheld as prescribed in Section 2.58 of Revenue Regulations 2-98. Also, the unremitted taxes amounting to ₱96,849.34 should be remitted immediately to the BIR.

We have recommended that management submit immediately the disbursement vouchers and liquidations amounting to P164,616.73 and P88,200.00, together with all the supporting documents to the Auditor for  verification, otherwise, management must cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of officials concerned until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against such official or employee pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445.
